title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: National Weather Service reports widespread showers and thunderstorms with 1-3 inches of rain, leading to potential flooding.
A High Wind Warning is issued for eastern Massachusetts with gusts expected to reach 45-65 mph tonight.
Meteorologists advise preparations for potential flooding as river levels may rise midweek due to heavy rain.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest while battling a warehouse fire in Hyde Park, Boston on Sunday night.
Firefighters responded to the blaze at 1 Westinghouse Plaza, with the fire causing approximately $750,000 in damages.
The Boston Fire Investigation Unit is investigating the cause of the fire; no other injuries were reported.

title: Mass. native’s 44-point performance helped Penn reach NCAA Tournament in dramatic fashion
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: T.J. Power scored 44 points to lead Penn to an 88-84 overtime win over Yale in the Ivy League final.
Penn's victory over Yale secured an automatic NCAA Tournament bid; Penn will face Illinois as a 14-seed.
Power transferred from Duke and Virginia to Penn this season and set an Ivy League conference tournament record.

title: Hundreds of flights delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport experienced over 300 flight delays and cancellations on Monday due to severe weather conditions on the East Coast.
Massport issued a high wind warning with potential gusts reaching 70 mph, advising passengers to check flight statuses.
Delta, Southwest, American, and United Airlines are permitting itinerary changes without fees under specific conditions.

title: 11 charged after allegedly staging robberies to claim victim status on immigration applications
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Eleven Indian nationals charged in visa fraud scheme involving staged robberies for immigration benefits, says U.S. Attorney's Office.
Arrests made in Massachusetts; defendants also face charges in Kentucky, Missouri, and Ohio, pending Boston federal proceedings.
Rambhai Patel and Balwinder Singh organized robberies; Patel sentenced to 20 months, fined $850,000 for his role.

title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: One person was injured in a shooting on Sunday evening in Boston's South End.
The incident occurred near Washington Street and East Berkeley Street at 7:42 p.m.
Police have made no arrests yet and the investigation is ongoing.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony is emerging as a superstar at World Baseball Classic
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony helped Team USA win 2-1 against the Dominican Republic with a game-winning home run in the 2026 WBC.
The 21-year-old outfielder is batting .318 with two home runs and seven RBIs through six games in the World Baseball Classic.
Anthony played 71 MLB games for the Red Sox in 2025, batting .292 before an injury, and is expected to lead in 2026.

title: 5 Boston sports moments that just missed out on the Soundbites Bracket
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rob Gronkowski's 'Yo soy fiesta' quote from 2012 AFC Championship celebration missed inclusion in Boston.com's Soundbites Bracket.
Bill Belichick's 'I'm not Mona Lisa Vito of the football world' quote during Deflategate narrowly excluded from the bracket.
Dustin Pedroia's 'Laser show' defense of David Ortiz in 2010 is another notable Boston sports quote left out of the bracket.

title: Oscars 2026: The best jokes from Conan O’Brien’s monologue
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien hosted the Oscars for the second year, delivering a mix of high-concept jokes and pre-taped sketches.
O'Brien's monologue included jabs at Netflix, Timothee Chalamet, and a fictional Oscars hosted by Waymo and Kid Rock.
Jimmy Kimmel contributed politically charged jokes while presenting, referencing the Melania Trump documentary and CBS.

title: Fundraiser: Community remembers Danvers mother, nurse killed in her home
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Janet Swallow, a 68-year-old nurse from Danvers, was allegedly killed by Anthony DeMayo, an 18-year-old high school senior.
Police report that DeMayo admitted to planning the attack, breaking into Swallow's home, and stabbing her while she slept.
A GoFundMe for Swallow's family has raised nearly $50,000, aiming for $65,000 to help with financial burdens after the tragedy.
